---
title: "AI-Native"
subtitle: "How I Built a Software Factory with AI Agents"
---

# 31 Days. 31 Products. One AI Agent. {.unnumbered}

**📍 Current Status: April 10, 2026 — Build narrative in progress, integrity tooling hardened**

This book is a real-time record of my attempt to build a software company using AI agents as the primary engineering team.

**The Plan**: From March 1 to March 31, 2026, build one product every day. My only engineering resource is an AI agent named Jarvis — Claude running on OpenClaw, talking to me through Telegram, with access to my servers, my code, and my deployment pipelines.

**What you're reading**: A work-in-progress book that gets updated from real build sessions. No retrospective hero fiction. No post-hoc cleanup that pretends it happened earlier. Just real events documented as they occur, including the maintenance work required to keep the manuscript trustworthy.

**Part I** covers the setup period (February 2026) — getting the agent running, solving the first real problems, and preparing for the sprint.  
**Part II** covers the March build sprint and its aftermath as honestly as the repo can support.  
**Part III** captures the patterns and lessons once the daily build record is stable enough to synthesize.

## Who This Book Is For

- Developers who want to understand how to work with AI agents on real projects
- Founders who want to see what "AI-native" means in practice, not in theory
- Anyone who wants an honest account of what works and what does not

## How to Read This Book (Work in Progress)

**Part I** should be read in order. It explains the tools, the setup, and the context for the 31-day sprint.

**Part II** is uneven on purpose because the underlying work was uneven. Some days are fully documented. Some are still placeholders waiting for honest rewrites rather than invented stories.

**Part III** is where the broader lessons will land once the daily chapters stop moving so much.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Following the Progress
Check out different git commits to read the book as it existed on any given day. The repo-level audit scripts also record what is still missing, broken, or blocked on local tooling.
:::

This repo now includes manuscript integrity checks for placeholders, repo-local links, image references, and render-environment readiness. Those checks are not a substitute for `quarto render`, but they make it much harder to accidentally claim progress without leaving behind evidence.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Source Code
All code lives at [github.com/venkatesh3007](https://github.com/venkatesh3007). Each chapter links to the relevant repositories and commits.
:::
